title: Checkpointed OpenCode Event-Log Compaction
pipeline_rationale: Checkpoint/replay semantics and mutation of shared persistent storage create high data-loss risk. Independent review, RED/GREEN tests, disposable-copy rehearsal, bounded approval, validation, and documentation are mandatory.

skipped_stages.rationale: No breaking-change or complexity (B+C) trigger. Track classified as certain/high-risk but no architectural ambiguity requiring Stage 3 diversity review. Checkpoint/replay risk addressed by RED/GREEN gate (Stage 2/4) and disposable-copy rehearsal (Stage 5).

test_framework: bun:test (primary, source feature @ packages/core) + PowerShell PSScriptAnalyzer/syntax harness (skill scripts) + disposable SQLite fixture checks
summary: Build or adopt an upstream-compatible checkpointed event-log compactor and package a fail-closed reusable lazy-vault skill, preserving visible chats and current projections while reclaiming superseded event history older than 90 days.
keyOutcomes: Checkpointed, replay-safe, append-safe bounded event compaction
Reusable opencode-event-log-compactor lazy-vault skill and scripts
Disposable-copy proof before exact-hash-approved live execution
Measured logical and physical space savings with rollback
risks: Projection or replay divergence after deleting durable events
Sequence/checkpoint incompatibility with future appends or sync
Concurrent writer or SQLite swap corruption
Sensitive content or identifiers leaking through manifests and logs
Upstream PR design changing before implementation
dependencies: Execution-time status of anomalyco/opencode PR #36710 or equivalent release
Isolated upstream OpenCode source checkout/fork if feature remains unreleased
Adequate disk space for fresh backup, disposable copy, and VACUUM INTO candidate
Explicit exact manifest SHA-256 approval before live apply
deliverables: Upstream-compatible OpenCode event-log compaction source feature or released-CLI adoption
Versioned checkpoint/manifest format and compatibility matrix
Fixture, source, skill, and disposable-copy test evidence
Backup, validation, rollback, and exact savings reports
validationCriteria: Current projections, visible chats, replay, and subsequent append remain equivalent
Owned/synced/workspace and ambiguous aggregates fail closed
Live apply is bounded, transaction-safe, exact-hash approved, and backed up
VACUUM INTO candidate passes integrity/application checks before reversible swap
Skill passes structural and functional validation
blockers: Phase 6 HARD STOP: no authorization for live DB mutation. Requires exact manifest-hash approval, maintenance window, and explicit go/no-go.

Task 4.8: skill_find/skill_use cannot discover the new skill in-session (lazy-vault index built at session start). Structural validation passes (9/9 scripts valid, WhatIf recognized, 0 content leaks). Discovery/activation requires session restart.

RESOLVED (Stage 9 closeout): Tasks 5.4-5.7 now [x] - satisfied via synthetic-fixture tests (plan 5.4-5.7 equivalence notes; acceptance suite 51 tests pass). Production disposable copy had 0 age-eligible candidates; operational go/no-go NO-GO. No longer blocking.

Production-scale performance note: full-scan dry-run on 22 GiB / 720 aggregates takes ~16 min (msg.updated: 418s, msg.part.updated: ~550s). Early termination applies when candidates exist. Current database has 0 age-eligible candidates (all events < 90 days old).
test_evidence_current: 56 pass / 0 fail; 118 expect calls; typecheck clean; lint 0 warnings / 0 errors (includes 5 cutoffDays tests added 2026-07-18)
lint_status: clean: 0 warnings, 0 errors. Stage 9 closeout resolved the 2 prior no-unsafe-type-assertion warnings in event-log-compaction.ts (projectedData JSON.parse + checkFreeSpace statfs) via runtime type guards (isStringRecord).
closeout_verdict: Closed with minor follow-ups (authorization boundary). Deliverable (source, tests, skill) complete and validated; live apply (Phase 6/7) deferred pending exact manifest-hash approval, maintenance window, writer shutdown, and go/no-go.
